title: Python EDHOC: Signature_2 verification uses wrong verify() signature
description: In python/src/lichen/crypto/edhoc.py line 342, the code calls verify_key.verify(m_2, signature_2). However, nacl.signing.VerifyKey.verify() expects the signature to be prepended to the message, not as a separate argument. The correct usage is verify_key.verify(signature_2 + m_2) or verify_key.verify(smessage) where smessage is the signed message containing the signature prefix. The current code will always raise BadSignatureError.
close_reason: False positive: nacl.signing.VerifyKey.verify() does support a separate signature parameter for detached signature verification. The call verify_key.verify(m_2, signature_2) is correct - m_2 is the message and signature_2 is the detached signature.
